title: auricula-ursi deorum
intro: Auricula-ursi deorum, also known as bearberry or kinnikinnick, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe, North America, and Asia. The medicinal properties of auricula-ursi deorum are attributed to its high concentration of arbutin, a glycoside that acts as a natural astringent and antiseptic, making it effective in treating urinary tract infections and other conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to alleviate symptoms of kidney stones, urinary incontinence, and certain types of diarrhea, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of auricula-ursi deorum can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote urinary health and overall well-being.
